---
title: "Direct CTmax/z 4PL: why `~ 0 + group` (cell-means) not `~ 1 + group` (treatment)"
date: 2026-06-22
format: html
---

**Question (DN).** In the direct parameterisation, what happens if we fit
`ctmax = ~ 1 + species` (treatment/reference coding, = the default `~ species`)
instead of `ctmax = ~ 0 + species` (cell-means coding)?

## Short answer

It is the **same statistical model** тАФ identical likelihood, fitted surface, and
predictions, so `tls(wf, by = "species")` returns the same per-species z/CTmax
and the same contrasts either way (it works off `posterior_linpred`, not the raw
coefficients). What changes is (1) what `summary()` shows and, more importantly,
(2) **which priors get applied to what**. The package's default direct-mode
priors are built for **cell-means** coding; under treatment coding they land on
the wrong quantities. **Use `~ 0 + species`.**

## The two concrete problems with `~ 1 + species`

`make_4pl_priors()` recognises a group term only when it is cell-means coded
(`^0 + G`, via `cell_means_coefs()`). Generating the priors both ways on a toy
3-species dataset:

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
suppressMessages(pkgload::load_all(here::here(), quiet = TRUE))
set.seed(1)
raw <- expand.grid(species = c("Md","Rp","Sa"), temp = c(34,36,38),
                   duration = c(10,60,240))
raw$n <- 30L; raw$alive <- rbinom(nrow(raw), 30, 0.6)
d <- standardize_data(raw, temp = "temp", duration = "duration",
                      n_total = "n", n_surv = "alive", duration_unit = "minutes")
d$species <- factor(raw$species)

rows <- function(p) as.data.frame(p)[as.data.frame(p)$nlpar %in%
                      c("upraw","CTmaxdev","logz"), c("prior","coef","nlpar")]
rows(make_4pl_priors(d, ctmax = ~ 0 + species, z = ~ 0 + species))  # cell-means
rows(make_4pl_priors(d, ctmax = ~ species,     z = ~ species))      # treatment
```

Observed output:

```
CELL-MEANS  ~ 0 + species
  normal(3.23, 1)   speciesMd   upraw     <- each species' asymptote centred
  normal(3.23, 1)   speciesRp   upraw
  normal(3.23, 1)   speciesSa   upraw
  normal(0, 0.5)                upraw
  normal(0, 10)                 CTmaxdev  <- per-species (no intercept)
  normal(1.10, 0.7)             logz      <- per-species, centred at log(3): zтЙИ3

TREATMENT   ~ species  (= 1 + species)
  normal(3.23, 1)   Intercept   upraw     <- ONLY the reference level centred
  normal(0, 0.5)                upraw     <- Rp, Sa asymptotes: generic dev prior
  normal(0, 10)                 CTmaxdev  <- now on Intercept AND the contrasts
  normal(1.10, 0.7)             logz      <- now on Intercept AND the contrasts
```

1. **Asymptote centring is lost for non-reference groups.** The centred
   `upraw`/`lowraw`/`logk` priors are applied per level under cell-means, but only
   to the `Intercept` under treatment coding тАФ so Rp and Sa keep only the generic
   `normal(0, 0.5)` deviation prior. This centring is *load-bearing*: a flat
   asymptote prior produced 191 divergences
   (`notes/2026-06-21-prior-sensitivity-direct-ctmax-z.qmd`). Treatment coding
   reintroduces that fragility for every non-reference group.

2. **The `logz` prior becomes a wrong prior on the contrast.** `normal(log 3,
   0.7)` is meant to say "each species' z тЙИ 3 ┬░C". Under cell-means it does.
   Under treatment coding the contrast coefficients (`speciesRp = logz_Rp тИТ
   logz_Md`) *also* get `normal(log 3, 0.7)` тАФ i.e. the prior now expects each
   non-reference species' z to be **тЙИ 3├Ч the reference species' z**, instead of
   centred on "no difference" (0). That biases the species sensitivity contrasts.
   (`CTmaxdev`'s `normal(0, 10)` is wide enough that the analogous effect on the
   CTmax contrasts is mild, but still conceptually a prior on a difference rather
   than an absolute.)

## Recommendation

- Keep **`ctmax = ~ 0 + species`, `z = ~ 0 + species`** тАФ each group's CTmax/z is
  a parameter with the correct, symmetric informative prior, and `summary()`
  reads the per-species values directly.
- For reference-vs-other **contrasts**, don't switch coding тАФ difference the
  per-group posterior draws from `tls(wf, by = "species")` (correct priors,
  correct contrast, with pMCMC).
- The `0 + G` machinery (`cell_means_coefs`, `resolve_shape`) is the package's
  intended path for grouped direct-parameterisation fits; the tests use it.

## Aside (caught while testing)

`library(bayesTLS)` (installed) does **not** yet have the direct-mode `ctmax`/`z`
arguments тАФ that code is in the working tree only. The new case-study scaffold
(`ms/case_studies_new.qmd`) uses them, so the package must be **reinstalled**
(or chunks run via `pkgload::load_all()`) before those chunks will run. See
[[project_render_uses_installed_package]].
